perturbation.original_text: 1.22. PaperExchange Revenue shall mean the gross revenue received by PaperExchange from Transaction Fees less (a) credits for claims, allowances, retroactive price reductions or returned goods, and (b) sales, excise, use, value-added and other similar taxes (excluding income taxes) actually paid.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.22. PaperExchange Revenue shall mean the gross revenue received by PaperExchange from Transaction Fees less (a) credits for claims, allowances, retroactive price reductions or returned goods, and (b) sales, excise, use, value-added and other similar taxes (excluding income taxes) actually paid. For the purposes of revenue sharing with VerticalNet, PaperExchange Revenue will mean total income without subtracting any credits or taxes.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of PaperExchange Revenue is modified to include a contradictory statement that for revenue sharing, credits and taxes should not be subtracted. This creates ambiguity because the original definition explicitly excludes these items. The in-text contradiction is introduced in the definition of a key financial term, which causes uncertainty in calculations of revenue sharing with VerticalNet, in article 7.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.4. Revenue Sharing. After PaperExchange has generated PaperExchange Revenue equal to *****, PaperExchange shall pay an amount equal to ***** of the PaperExchange Revenue to VerticalNet; provided, however, that if, in any given calendar year, VerticalNet receives ***** pursuant to this Section 7.4 [Revenue Sharing], the percentage of PaperExchange Revenue that PaperExchange shall pay to VerticalNet for the remainder of such calendar year shall be reduced to *****; and provided further, however, that if, in any given calendar year, VerticalNet receives ***** in the aggregate pursuant to this Section 7.4 [Revenue Sharing], the percentage of PaperExchange Revenue that PaperExchange shall pay to VerticalNet for the remainder of such calendar year shall be reduced to *****.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'PaperExchange Revenue' by adding a contradictory statement that for revenue sharing with VerticalNet, credits and taxes should not be subtracted, while the original definition explicitly excludes these items. This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict with section 7.4, which refers to 'PaperExchange Revenue' for revenue sharing calculations. The change introduces an in-text contradiction because section 7.4 relies on a consistent definition of PaperExchange Revenue. The altered definition now has two meanings depending on whether it's being calculated for PaperExchange's internal accounting or for revenue sharing with VerticalNet. This misalignment directly affects the payment obligations outlined in section 7.4.

perturbation.original_text:
3. VERTICALNET CONTENT

3.1. VerticalNet shall provide or make available to PaperExchange, for use in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, (a) the full text of all original content (headlines, feature articles, columns and case studies) created from time to time by the Managing Editor of Pulp and Paper Online, and (b) the content created from time to time by guest columnists for Pulp and Paper Online, to the extent such columnists have approved the provision of such content by VerticalNet to PaperExchange. VerticalNet shall provide such content (the "VerticalNet Content") to PaperExchange twice per week, in two "batches" of the VerticalNet Content created or acquired since the last provision of VerticalNet Content by VerticalNet to PaperExchange.
perturbation.changed_text:
3. VERTICALNET CONTENT

3.1. VerticalNet may provide or make available to PaperExchange, for use in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, (a) a summary of all original content (headlines, feature articles, columns and case studies) created from time to time by the Managing Editor of Pulp and Paper Online, and (b) the content created from time to time by guest columnists for Pulp and Paper Online, to the extent such columnists have approved the provision of such content by VerticalNet to PaperExchange. VerticalNet shall provide such content (the "VerticalNet Content") to PaperExchange once per week, in a single "batch" of the VerticalNet Content created or acquired since the last provision of VerticalNet Content by VerticalNet to PaperExchange.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of VERTICALNET CONTENT is modified. The original version says that 'VerticalNet shall provide... the full text of all original content... twice per week', while the modified version changes this to 'VerticalNet may provide... a summary of all original content... once per week'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the nature of the content (full text vs summary) and the frequency of delivery. This affects the in-text obligations reffering on the content provided in article 3.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2. VerticalNet hereby grants to PaperExchange a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use, reproduce, display and transmit the VerticalNet Content, solely in connection with the development, maintenance and operation of the PaperExchange Site, subject to and in accordance with the terms, conditions and provisions of this Agreement. PaperExchange may 5 reproduce, display and transmit any VerticalNet Content for up to three weeks on the PaperExchange Site, and after the expiration of such three week period PaperExchange shall cease to reproduce, display and transmit such VerticalNet Content and remove such VerticalNet Content from the PaperExchange Site.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes "shall provide... the full text... twice per week" to "may provide... a summary... once per week." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, "PaperExchange may reproduce, display and transmit any VerticalNet Content for up to three weeks," implying a certain type and frequency of 'VerticalNet Content' as previously defined. The altered definition of VerticalNet Content and the frequency of its provision makes the clause in 3.2. regarding reproduction and display for up to three weeks contradictory, because the content is no longer provided at the same frequency or in the same format.

perturbation.original_text: 12.4. Indemnification by PaperExchange. PaperExchange shall indemnify and hold harmless VerticalNet and its officers, directors, employees and agents from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, obligations, penalties, judgments, awards, costs, expenses and disbursements, including without limitation, the costs, expenses and disbursements, as and when incurred, of investigating, preparing or defending any action, suit, proceeding or investigation asserted by a third party, caused by, relating to, based upon, arising out of or in connection with (a) any breach by PaperExchange of the representations, warranties or agreements made by it under this Agreement, (b) negligence, recklessness or intentional misconduct on the part of PaperExchange or its officers, directors, employees, agents or consultants, (c) any claim that the PaperExchange Content infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property Rights or any other right of any third party or (d) the promotion, advertisement or marketing of the VerticalNet Content or VerticalNet Archived Content by or on behalf of PaperExchange.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.4. Indemnification by PaperExchange. PaperExchange shall indemnify and hold harmless VerticalNet and its officers, directors, employees and agents from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, obligations, penalties, judgments, awards, costs, expenses and disbursements, including without limitation, the costs, expenses and disbursements, as and when incurred, of investigating, preparing or defending any action, suit, proceeding or investigation asserted by a third party, caused by, relating to, based upon, arising out of or in connection with (a) any breach by PaperExchange of the representations, warranties or agreements made by it under this Agreement, (b) gross negligence, recklessness or intentional misconduct on the part of PaperExchange or its officers, directors, employees, agents or consultants, (c) any claim that the PaperExchange Content infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property Rights or any other right of any third party or (d) the promotion, advertisement or marketing of the VerticalNet Content or VerticalNet Archived Content by or on behalf of PaperExchange.
perturbation.explanation: The original version in section 12.4(b) includes 'negligence' as one cause of action in which PaperExchange must indemnify VerticalNet. The modified version changes this to 'gross negligence'. This creates a higher threshold for liability, because negligence, in it's more basic form, is not actionable, where the previous version it was. This impacts the indemnification obligations in article 12.3 because the terms of negligience are being contracted. By changing 'negligence' to 'gross negligence,' the original obligation has been changed, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.3. Limitation of Liability. EXCEPT IN CONNECTION WITH A BREACH BY EITHER PARTY OF ARTICLE 10, THE INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OF PAPEREXCHANGE UNDER SECTIONS 12.4(c) [Indemnification by PaperExchange] AND THE INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OF VERTICALNET UNDER SECTION 12.5(c) [Indemnification by VerticalNet], NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, EXEMPLARY OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, HOWEVER CAUSED AND UNDER ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. EXCEPT IN CONNECTION WITH A BREACH BY EITHER PARTY OF ARTICLE 10, THE INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OF PAPEREXCHANGE UNDER SECTION 12.4(c) [Indemnification by PaperExchange] AND THE INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OF VERTICALNET UNDER SECTION 12.5(c) [Indemnification by VerticalNet], EACH PARTY'S LIABILITY FOR DAMAGES HEREUNDER SHALL NOT EXCEED $1,000,000.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 12.4 included 'negligence' as a cause of action for indemnification by PaperExchange. The changed text raises the standard to 'gross negligence.' Section 12.3 limits liability except for certain breaches and indemnification obligations, including those under 12.4(c). It explicitly mentions that this limitation applies 'under any theory of liability (including negligence)'. By changing 'negligence' to 'gross negligence' in section 12.4, the indemnification obligation is narrowed and creates a direct conflict with section 12.3, because the limitation of liability doesn't include gross negligence anymore. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
